goal: Hub Founder/Team Health: dashboard restructure with team-health summary, review aging, risk radar, intent coverage, decision hotspots, weekly digest

summary.title: Hub Founder/Team Health dashboard — must-have acceptance criteria
summary.what: Restructures the Hub dashboard per the team-health spec. New types (HubTeamHealth + HubCoverageSummary + HubRiskRadar + HubWeeklyDigest) computed by a pure buildTeamHealth function. Dashboard renders: team-health banner with three-bucket health classifier and partial-data honesty when coverage is unavailable; 4 metric cards with aging tags; Needs attention + Decision hotspots split (Hot files renamed); three-card row Coverage / Review aging / Risk radar; weekly digest with sealed/proposed/abandoned counts + important decisions + risks to watch; Recent intents table at the bottom. Spec-mandated copy test forbids productivity/leaderboard/performance/velocity language.
summary.why: Per spec: lift Hub from 'human view of local intent history' to 'founder/team-readable intent health dashboard'. Founders need a one-glance answer to 'is the team's intent memory healthy', and the dashboard needed an explanation layer over the raw counts. Coverage stays partial-data-honest until the gaps engine is wired through; risk-missing-mitigation stays nil-able until the heuristic earns its place — spec is explicit on both.

decisions.chose: when Coverage.Available=false the HealthSummary prefixes Coverage data unavailable rather than reading as healthy
decisions.rationale: spec §4.3: data unavailability never reads as healthy without saying so

decisions.chose: spec §14 mandatory copy test: rendered template must not contain productivity/performance/leaderboard/velocity
decisions.rationale: guards against future edits silently turning Hub into a manager-monitoring panel

decisions.chose: pointer-with-nil-as-unavailable so the renderer hides the line when the heuristic isnt available
decisions.rationale: spec §8.2: heuristic is fragile; opt-in once it earns its place

decisions.chose: ship items 1-8 of spec implementation order; defer /coverage.html, /digest.html, actor activity, lifecycle health, mainline digest CLI
decisions.rationale: each of those is on the optional list in §15 acceptance criteria; ship the load-bearing dashboard now, iterate on dedicated pages once the dogfood signal arrives

risks: Risk-heavy file detection currently uses 'has any risk text' as the proxy; HubIntent doesn't carry anti_patterns directly today (lives in domain.IntentSummary). Feature works, but the count understates: an intent that records anti_patterns but no risks would be invisible to the radar. Followup: surface anti_patterns on HubIntent.

anti_patterns.what: Adding productivity / leaderboard / performance / velocity language to the dashboard
anti_patterns.why: spec §1.3 forbids this explicitly; the test in TestHubDashboard_DoesNotUseLeaderboardLanguage will fail. The Hub is for team health, not individual evaluation.

anti_patterns.what: Marking team-health healthy when coverage data is unavailable
anti_patterns.why: spec §4.3: dashboard never reads as healthy without saying which inputs are missing. populateHealthLevel always prepends Coverage data unavailable when Coverage.Available=false.

anti_patterns.what: Computing dashboard metrics inside the HTML template
anti_patterns.why: spec §13: Go layer builds the view model, template renders. Putting score logic in template prevents reuse and testing.

followups: /coverage.html dedicated page using HubCoverageSummary once gaps engine is wired through
Actor activity section (carefully avoiding leaderboard framing)
Lifecycle health card (median time to seal / median time in review)
Surface anti_patterns directly on HubIntent so Risk radar can include them
Spec §11 follow-up: per-actor view that sorts by needs-attention not productivity
